REPORTS FROM THE BROADCAST MEDIA, NEWSPAPER AND SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT THAT FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED IN THE MALIBU APARTMENTS ALONG MALIBU DRIVE BETWEEN MIDDLETON STREET AND GREEN STREET. 2 TO 3 INCHES OF RAIN FELL IN JUST OVER 1 HOUR. FLOODING OCCURRED IN 10 UNITS WITH WATER LEVELS REACHING 1 TO 2 FEET. CARS THAT WERE PARKED WERE ALSO FLOODED. A SMALL CREEK RUNS THROUGH THE COMPLEX AND LATER EMPTIES INTO SUNNYSIDE CANAL. THE NEARBY ORANGEBURG WATER PLANT REPORTED 3.00 INCHES, A WEATHER OBSERVER ALONG MASON DRIVER REPORTED 3.37 INCHES AND A COCORAHS OBSERVER OFF JACKSON DRIVE REPORTED 2.41 INCHES. SUNNYSIDE CANAL ROSE FROM 0.54 FEET UP TO 5.31 FEET IN 45 MINUTES, CRESTED AT 215 AM.

ROADWAY FLOODED AT THE INTERSECTION OF WHALEY STREET AND MAIN STREET. USGS WEBCAM SHOWS A FORD MUSTANG WAS SWEPT OFF THE ROADWAY BY FLOODWATERS AT 5:25 PM. THE STREAM GAGE REGISTERED 7.57 FEET AT 5:15 PM. FLOOD STAGE IS 7.2 FEET.

USGS STREAM GAGE LOCATED AT THE INTERSECTION OF MAIN STREET AND WHALEY STREET RECORDED A PEAK LEVEL OF 10.11 FEET AT 5:37 PM. FLOOD STAGE IS 7.2 FEET. WATERS ARE NOW RECEDING FROM THE INTERSECTION BASED ON VIDEO FROM THE CO-LOCATED USGS WEBCAM.

WAYNESBORO FIRE CHIEF REPORTED ABOUT 5 TO 7 HOMES IN THE NORTHERN PART OF TOWN WERE FLOODED WITH ABOUT A HALF FOOT OF WATER. ABOUT 3 TO 5 VEHICLES WERE FLOODED. 2 PERSONS WERE RESCUED FROM A FLOODED VEHICLE. NO INJURIES. TIME ESTIMATED.
WAYNESBORO FIRE CHIEF REPORTS 2 PERSONS WERE RESCUED FROM A FLOODED COMPACT CAR ON E 7TH ST AND FULCHER ST.
